item.title: Micro-Scale DMLS Tolerances: Practical Limits at Plus-or-Minus 0.001 Inch
item.description: Micro-scale DMLS achieves tolerances an order of magnitude tighter than standard process settings. This guide quantifies what drives dimensional accuracy and its practical limits.

item.title: About Micro 3D Print
item.description:
Precision Metal Printing at the Micro Scale

Micro 3D Print covers the precision ceiling of metal additive manufacturing — DMLS parameter sets optimized for 50 µm layers, 80 µm laser spot diameter, and feature resolution approaching 0.15 mm (0.006 inch). Where standard DMLS optimizes for build rate and part size, micro-DMLS optimizes for accuracy and minimum feature size.

Applications: watchmaking (movement bridges, crown components), surgical instruments (laparoscopic tips, implantable device housings), microfluidics (stainless channels ≥ 0.3 mm), precision sensors, and miniaturized electronics housings.

item.title: Services
item.description:
Micro-Scale DMLS

Micro-scale DMLS pushes the dimensional limits of the process: 50 µm layer thickness, 80 µm laser spot size, and parameter sets optimized for fine features rather than build rate. Achievable: 0.006 inch (0.15 mm) wall thickness, ±0.001 inch (±0.025 mm) tolerance on features < 10 mm. Materials: Ti6Al4V, 17-4 PH, 316L, CoCrMo. Applications: watchmaking, precision instruments, MEMS housings, microfluidic manifolds, dental prosthetics, minimally invasive surgical tools.

Microfluidics & Lab

Metal microfluidic components require the chemical inertness of stainless or titanium combined with feature resolution below 0.5 mm. DMLS at micro-scale parameters produces internal channels ≥ 0.3 mm diameter, multi-layer channel networks, and integrated fittings. Applications: microreactors, high-pressure fluid distribution, analytical instrument sample handling, and implantable drug delivery device housings.
